results.owner_or_reason: coverage-index has no direct hit, but plan.json+bodies match: [wiki-api] Epic E1 '[EPIC] wiki-api: Phase-0 dual-surface strangler facade & Go runtime foundation' cites 'Arch A-STRANGLER' verbatim (bodies/E1.md); E1-S2 'Byte-compatible /api/orvex/* reverse proxy (surface a)' (FR-A15, D-A9) -> ENG-2512, E1-S3 'Draft /v1 verb-grammar surface scaffold served alongside (surface b)' (FR-A15, D-A9) -> ENG-2513

Epic E1 title includes 'Go runtime foundation'; E1-S1 'Go service skeleton, six-tier layout, config' (FR-A15, NFR-A2) -> ENG-2511; E1-S6 'Blocking CI bootstrap gates ... + no-AGPL-import guard' (NFR-A7, the AGPL-contamination reason A-RUNTIME rejects TS) -> ENG-2516

'the human wiki never touches wiki-api (D-A2)' is a design-rationale sentence in §5 Risks (justifies plain-Deployment topology), carries no FR/NFR tag, is not cited by id in any story body (grep across breakdown/wiki-api/bodies/*.md: zero hits), and is not among the four service-scoped ADRs the SE-Arch review's own B5 finding lists to file (A-RUNTIME/D-A11, A-STATE/D-A10, A-DESCRIPTOR/D-A7, A-VERBS/FR-A6). It is an emergent consequence of wiki-api being agent-only (no human-facing routes exist in the /v1 or /api/orvex/* surfaces built under E1/E3), not a discrete buildable requirement

cited only paired with D-A9 in the A-STRANGLER heading itself ('dual-surface facade first, relocation behind it (D-A3, D-A9)') — same underlying decision as A-STRANGLER; covered by the same E1 epic (ENG-2511..2516) plus the phased-relocation language repeated in bodies/E2.md, E4.md ('later phases swap handlers... one seam at a time')

explicitly superseded in the page body: 'A-ASK — no ask here... (D-A12, supersedes D-A4)' — D-A4 is a dead/replaced decision (the earlier ask-routing choice), not a build target; the live decision D-A12 is COVERED via A-ASK/E4-S4 (ENG-2530)
